wines.name: Vina Tondonia Reserva
wines.producer_name: R. Lopez de Heredia Vina Tondonia
wines.oak_regime: Around 6 years in American oak barrels, racked by gravity twice a year, then long bottle ageing

taste.summary: The reference traditional Rioja Reserva: pale brick colour, oxidative dried-fruit and balsamic aromatics over a fine-boned, high-acid frame that drinks like old Burgundy.

pairings.why: The wine's silky old-oak savour wraps around vine-roasted lamb without overwhelming it.
Game and the Reserva's autumn-leaf and dried-cherry notes are a classic register match.
Acorn-fed ham's nutty fat lifts the wine's walnut and cured-meat savour.
Basque smoked cheese mirrors the wine's mellow oxidative edge.

history.summary: Vina Tondonia is the 100-hectare estate vineyard Rafael Lopez de Heredia planted from 1913 above the Ebro at Haro. The Reserva is aged for years in the bodega's own American-oak barrels and released only when the family judges it ready, often a decade or more after the harvest.

milestones.event: Vina Tondonia vineyard fully planted above the Ebro
Estate bottling of Tondonia under the family label established
Tim Atkin and Decanter repeatedly cite Tondonia as the benchmark traditional Rioja

wines.description: Benchmark traditional Rioja Reserva from the Tondonia estate vineyard at Haro, aged years in American oak. High-acid, dried-fruit and balsamic style built for the long haul.

wines.name: Vina Tondonia Gran Reserva
wines.producer_name: R. Lopez de Heredia Vina Tondonia
wines.oak_regime: Around 9 years in American oak barrels with biannual racking, then extended bottle ageing before release

taste.summary: Released only in great years and only after a decade-plus in the cellar; ethereal, savoury and impossibly fresh, the Gran Reserva is one of the wine world's great old-style reds.

pairings.why: Mature game meets the Gran Reserva's truffle and forest-floor depth.
Slow-roast lamb's sweet fat is the historic foil for aged Tondonia.
Crystalline hard sheep cheese matches the wine's saline, nutty finish.
A glass of old Tondonia turns a Donostia pintxo crawl into an occasion.

history.summary: The Gran Reserva is made only in vintages the family considers worthy and is held in barrel for about nine years and then for many more in bottle. Recent commercial releases have been from the 2000s while the cellar still holds decades of library stock.

milestones.event: Early Tondonia Gran Reserva vintages laid down
1981 becomes a celebrated Gran Reserva vintage
2010 released as a modern reference Gran Reserva

wines.description: Library-aged Gran Reserva from the Tondonia vineyard, held about nine years in American oak plus long bottle ageing. Ethereal, savoury and one of Rioja's great traditional reds.

wines.name: Vina Tondonia Blanco Reserva
wines.producer_name: R. Lopez de Heredia Vina Tondonia
wines.oak_regime: Around 6 years in American oak barrels with biannual racking before bottle ageing

taste.summary: A barrel-aged white Rioja with cult status: deeply oxidative, nutty and waxy, with piercing acidity that keeps it electric for decades, often compared to white Burgundy and old Hunter Semillon.

pairings.why: Salt cod in its emulsified oil meets the wine's waxy texture and saline lift.
Hake cheeks in pil pil echo the white's nutty, gelatinous richness.
The oxidative white's depth handles roast poultry like an aged Chardonnay.
Baked spider crab's sweetness plays against the wine's quince and mineral.

history.summary: Made from Viura and Malvasia grown on the Tondonia estate and aged for around six years in American oak, the white Tondonia is one of the longest-lived white wines in the world and a benchmark for oxidative-style whites.

milestones.event: Early white Tondonia Reserva vintages
Aged white Rioja gains global cult following among sommeliers

wines.description: Cult barrel-aged white Rioja from the Tondonia estate, six years in American oak. Oxidative, nutty and waxy with piercing acidity and decades of life.

wines.name: Vina Tondonia Rosado Gran Reserva
wines.producer_name: R. Lopez de Heredia Vina Tondonia
wines.oak_regime: Around 4 years in American oak barrels before release

taste.summary: The most famous rosado in the world: barrel-aged for years, copper-coloured, savoury and saline, it drinks like a serious gastronomic white rather than a summer pink.

pairings.why: The anchovy, olive and guindilla skewer's salt and acid mirror the rosado's saline bite.
Fresh marinated anchovy and the wine's saline citrus are a Cantabrian-coast natural.
Cured ham with melon meets the rosado's dried-orange savour.
Saffron seafood rice finds a serious partner in this gastronomic rosado.

history.summary: Lopez de Heredia ages its rosado in American oak for around four years, an almost unheard-of treatment for a pink wine, producing a savoury, age-worthy rose that has become a sommelier cult bottle.

milestones.event: Barrel-aged rosado established in the Tondonia range
Tondonia rosado becomes a global sommelier-list staple

wines.description: The world's most famous barrel-aged rosado, four years in American oak. Copper-coloured, savoury and saline; a gastronomic pink rather than a summer one.

wines.name: Vina Bosconia Reserva
wines.producer_name: R. Lopez de Heredia Vina Tondonia
wines.oak_regime: Around 5 years in American oak barrels with biannual racking, then bottle ageing

taste.summary: The richer, more Burgundian sibling to Tondonia, from a warmer estate vineyard; rounder fruit and spice but still carrying the house's signature freshness and old-oak savour.

pairings.why: Vine-shoot-roasted suckling lamb is the canonical Rioja match; the wine's resolved tannins frame the sweet fat.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A thick aged-beef chop over embers meets the wine's savoury oak and grip.
Cured Iberian ham echoes the wine's dried-cherry and leather register.
Bosconia's clove and cocoa depth flatters mature game.

history.summary: Vina Bosconia is the warmer, Pinot-noir-like estate vineyard of the house. The Reserva is fuller and rounder than Tondonia but follows the same long American-oak ageing regime.

milestones.event: Vina Bosconia estate established as a separate cuvee
Bosconia recognised as the richer counterpoint to Tondonia

wines.description: The richer, more Burgundian sibling to Tondonia from a warmer estate vineyard, five years in American oak. Rounder fruit with the house's signature freshness.

wines.name: Vina Cubillo Crianza
wines.producer_name: R. Lopez de Heredia Vina Tondonia
wines.oak_regime: Around 3 years in American oak barrels with biannual racking

taste.summary: The house's entry red and arguably the best-value traditional Rioja Crianza: already complex, savoury and high-acid, drinking like many estates' Reserva.

pairings.why: Vine-shoot-roasted suckling lamb is the canonical Rioja match; the wine's resolved tannins frame the sweet fat.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A thick aged-beef chop over embers meets the wine's savoury oak and grip.
Cured Iberian ham echoes the wine's dried-cherry and leather register.

history.summary: Cubillo is the youngest red in the Lopez de Heredia range yet still spends about three years in oak, far longer than the legal Crianza minimum, making it a benchmark value traditional Rioja.

milestones.event: Vina Cubillo introduced as the house Crianza

wines.description: The house entry red and a benchmark value traditional Rioja, around three years in American oak. Savoury, high-acid and complex well beyond its Crianza tier.

wines.name: Vina Gravonia Crianza
wines.producer_name: R. Lopez de Heredia Vina Tondonia
wines.oak_regime: Around 4 years in American oak barrels before release

taste.summary: A pure-Viura oxidative white aged four years in oak, lighter than Tondonia Blanco but with the same waxy, nutty, saline signature and remarkable freshness for its age.

pairings.why: Salt cod's gelatinous oil meets Gravonia's waxy, saline frame.
Marinated anchovy and the wine's preserved-lemon savour echo the Basque coast.
Oven-roast hake handles the white's nutty oxidative depth.

history.summary: Gravonia is the pure-Viura white aged about four years in oak, a more affordable gateway to the house's oxidative white style.

milestones.event: Vina Gravonia introduced as the house Crianza white

wines.description: Pure-Viura oxidative white aged four years in oak, the affordable gateway to the house white style. Waxy, nutty and saline with remarkable freshness.

wines.name: Castillo Ygay Gran Reserva Especial
wines.oak_regime: Long ageing in a mix of American and French oak, often more than a decade, plus extended bottle age before release

taste.summary: The estate's flagship from the Ygay vineyard, released only in exceptional years after a decade-plus of ageing; the 2010 was named Wine Spectator Wine of the Year 2020, a savoury, classical Gran Reserva of great longevity.

pairings.why: Vine-roasted lamb is the historic match for this grand old-school Gran Reserva.
Game's gaminess meets the wine's truffle and tobacco depth.
Aged-beef chop frames the wine's fine tannin and savoury oak.
Smoked Basque sheep cheese mirrors the wine's cedar and balsamic.

history.summary: Castillo Ygay is the estate's prestige bottling from the oldest parcels of the Ygay vineyard, made only in exceptional vintages and aged for many years in oak and bottle. The 2010 was named Wine Spectator's Wine of the Year in 2020.

milestones.event: First Castillo Ygay vintage laid down
A legendary 1942 Castillo Ygay released after decades in cask
2010 vintage named Wine Spectator Wine of the Year

wines.description: The estate flagship from the Ygay vineyard, released only in great years after a decade-plus of ageing. The 2010 was Wine Spectator Wine of the Year 2020.

wines.name: Castillo Ygay Blanco Gran Reserva Especial
wines.oak_regime: Extended ageing in oak, often more than a decade, before release

taste.summary: An extraordinarily rare white Gran Reserva, released decades after the harvest; the 1986 was relaunched to acclaim, with profound nutty, waxy oxidative complexity and electric acidity.

pairings.why: A grand white fish dish for one of Spain's grandest old whites.
Hake cheeks in pil pil meet the wine's gelatinous, nutty depth.
Crystalline cheese sharpens the white's saline, honeyed finish.

history.summary: The white Castillo Ygay is among the rarest aged whites in Spain, held for many years before release. The relaunched 1986 vintage became a collector's wine.

milestones.event: Vintage later relaunched as a landmark aged white
1986 Blanco released to critical acclaim

wines.description: An extraordinarily rare white Gran Reserva released decades after harvest. Profound nutty, waxy oxidative complexity with electric acidity.

wines.name: Marques de Murrieta Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 21 to 24 months in mostly American oak

taste.summary: The estate's classically styled Reserva from the Ygay vineyard: ripe but savoury, with American-oak spice over a firm, fresh Tempranillo core. A consistent benchmark Reserva.

pairings.why: Vine-shoot-roasted suckling lamb is the canonical Rioja match; the wine's resolved tannins frame the sweet fat.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A thick aged-beef chop over embers meets the wine's savoury oak and grip.
Cured Iberian ham echoes the wine's dried-cherry and leather register.

history.summary: The estate Reserva draws on the Ygay vineyard and is aged around two years in oak, sitting between the value tier and the prestige Castillo Ygay.

milestones.event: Modern Murrieta Reserva style established under the Cebrian-Sagarriga era

wines.description: The estate's classically styled Reserva from the Ygay vineyard, around two years in oak. Ripe but savoury Tempranillo with American-oak spice.

wines.name: Capellania Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 18 to 24 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: A single-vineyard barrel-aged Viura that bridges modern and traditional white Rioja: citrus and nut over a mineral, ageworthy frame.

pairings.why: Salt cod and Capellania's waxy, mineral citrus are a Spanish-coast classic.
Oven hake handles the wine's oak-framed nutty depth.
Baked spider crab's sweetness lifts the wine's preserved-lemon savour.

history.summary: Capellania comes from a single Viura parcel on the Ygay estate and is barrel-aged, made to age for a decade or more.

milestones.event: Capellania established as the estate's age-worthy single-vineyard white

wines.description: Single-vineyard barrel-aged Viura bridging modern and traditional white Rioja. Citrus and nut over a mineral, ageworthy frame.

wines.name: Dalmau Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 18 to 20 months in new French oak

taste.summary: The estate's modern, French-oak-aged prestige red with a dash of Cabernet: darker, denser and more international in cut than the classic Reserva while keeping Rioja freshness.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A char-grilled aged-beef chop matches Dalmau's firm tannin and mocha oak.
Dark game and the wine's cassis-and-espresso density are a natural fit.
Hard sheep cheese balances the wine's grippy structure.

history.summary: Dalmau is the estate's modern prestige red introduced in the 1990s, built on a small Tempranillo parcel with Cabernet and Graciano and aged in new French oak.

milestones.event: First Dalmau vintage as the estate's modern flagship

wines.description: The estate's modern, French-oak prestige red with a dash of Cabernet. Darker and denser than the classic Reserva while keeping Rioja freshness.

wines.name: Primer Rosado
wines.oak_regime: Partial fermentation and ageing in French oak

taste.summary: A gastronomic single-varietal Mazuelo rose with partial oak, pale and savoury rather than sweet, made for the table.

pairings.why: The anchovy-olive-guindilla skewer's salt sharpens the rose's redcurrant lift.
A versatile dry rose for a varied pintxo spread.
Charred shellfish meets the wine's saline citrus.

history.summary: Primer is the estate's modern single-varietal Mazuelo rose, made with partial oak to give a serious, gastronomic style.

milestones.event: Primer Rosado launched as a single-varietal Mazuelo rose

wines.description: A gastronomic single-varietal Mazuelo rose with partial oak, pale and savoury rather than sweet. Made for the table.

wines.name: Gran Reserva 904
wines.oak_regime: Around 4 years in American oak with manual racking, then long bottle ageing

taste.summary: A modern classic Gran Reserva: four years in American oak give the house's signature sweet-spice and dill lift over a silky, savoury, ageworthy Tempranillo frame.

pairings.why: Roast lamb's sweet fat is the textbook match for 904's silky savour.
Game meets the wine's leather and dried-cherry depth.
Aged-beef chop frames the fine tannin.
Smoked sheep cheese mirrors the American-oak sweet spice.

history.summary: The 904 name dates to the house's 1904 anniversary blend. The modern Gran Reserva 904 is aged about four years in American oak and is one of the most consistent classic Gran Reservas in Rioja.

milestones.event: Anniversary blend that gives the 904 its name
Modern 904 Gran Reserva style established
2011 vintage widely rated among the top Gran Reservas of its year

wines.description: A modern classic Gran Reserva, four years in American oak. Signature sweet-spice and dill lift over a silky, savoury, ageworthy frame.

wines.name: Gran Reserva 890
wines.oak_regime: Around 6 years in American oak with manual racking, then very long bottle ageing

taste.summary: The house's pinnacle Gran Reserva, made only in exceptional years and aged around six years in oak; ethereal, sandalwood-scented and extraordinarily long, a benchmark for traditional Rioja.

pairings.why: Mature game and 890's truffle-and-sandalwood depth are a grand match.
Vine-roasted lamb for one of Rioja's greatest Gran Reservas.
Crystalline cheese sharpens the silky, savoury finish.

history.summary: The 890 commemorates the house's 1890 founding. Made only in the best years and aged around six years in American oak, it is one of Rioja's most revered Gran Reservas.

milestones.event: Founding year commemorated by the 890 name
Modern 890 Gran Reserva style established
2010 vintage acclaimed as a modern reference Gran Reserva

wines.description: The house pinnacle Gran Reserva, made only in great years and aged about six years in oak. Ethereal, sandalwood-scented and extraordinarily long.

wines.name: Vina Ardanza Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 3 years in American oak with manual racking, then bottle ageing

taste.summary: The house's most famous Reserva and a worldwide benchmark: Garnacha lends generous red fruit, American oak the signature dill and sweet spice, all over a fresh, savoury frame.

pairings.why: Vine-shoot-roasted suckling lamb is the canonical Rioja match; the wine's resolved tannins frame the sweet fat.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A thick aged-beef chop over embers meets the wine's savoury oak and grip.
Cured Iberian ham echoes the wine's dried-cherry and leather register.
Game finds the Garnacha-lifted Ardanza an ideal partner.

history.summary: Vina Ardanza has been the house's signature Reserva since the 1940s, distinctive for its Garnacha component and long American-oak ageing. Certain great years are bottled as Especial selections.

milestones.event: First Vina Ardanza Reserva
2001 Especial selection becomes a celebrated vintage

wines.description: The house's most famous Reserva and a worldwide benchmark. Garnacha lends generous red fruit, American oak the signature dill and sweet spice.

wines.name: Vina Alberdi Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 2 years in American oak, with around 12 months in barrels previously used for the Gran Reservas

taste.summary: Labelled Reserva but styled as the house's accessible everyday red: pure Tempranillo with the signature American-oak lift, supple and savoury and a perennial value pick.

pairings.why: Vine-shoot-roasted suckling lamb is the canonical Rioja match; the wine's resolved tannins frame the sweet fat.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A thick aged-beef chop over embers meets the wine's savoury oak and grip.
Cured Iberian ham echoes the wine's dried-cherry and leather register.

history.summary: Vina Alberdi is the house's entry Reserva, a pure-Tempranillo wine aged about two years in oak and consistently cited as one of Rioja's best-value Reservas.

milestones.event: Vina Alberdi established as the house entry Reserva

wines.description: Labelled Reserva but styled as the house everyday red: pure Tempranillo with the signature American-oak lift. A perennial value pick.

wines.name: Vina Arana Gran Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 3 years in American and French oak, then long bottle ageing

taste.summary: Reborn as a Gran Reserva, Vina Arana is the more elegant, fresher and more savoury of the house's classic pair, with a touch of French oak adding finesse.

pairings.why: Vine-shoot-roasted suckling lamb is the canonical Rioja match; the wine's resolved tannins frame the sweet fat.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A thick aged-beef chop over embers meets the wine's savoury oak and grip.
Cured Iberian ham echoes the wine's dried-cherry and leather register.
Lighter game meets Arana's elegant, savoury frame.

history.summary: Once a Reserva, Vina Arana was relaunched as a Gran Reserva in the 2010s, positioned as the more elegant counterpoint to the fruitier Ardanza.

milestones.event: Vina Arana established
Relaunched as a Gran Reserva

wines.description: Reborn as a Gran Reserva, the more elegant and savoury of the house's classic pair, with a touch of French oak adding finesse.

wines.name: Imperial Gran Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 24 to 30 months in French and American oak, then long bottle ageing

taste.summary: Made only from Rioja Alta fruit in the best years; the 2004 was the first Spanish wine named Wine Spectator Wine of the Year in 2013. Classical, savoury and long.

pairings.why: Vine-roast lamb is the historic match for this grand Gran Reserva.
Game's depth meets the wine's tobacco and dried cherry.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: An aged-beef chop frames the wine's fine tannin and savoury oak.
Smoked Basque cheese mirrors the cedar and balsamic.

history.summary: Imperial began as a magnum bottling for the British market and became CVNE's prestige red, made only from Rioja Alta fruit in top years. The 2004 was Wine Spectator's Wine of the Year in 2013, the first Spanish wine so honoured.

milestones.event: Imperial established as a prestige bottling
2004 vintage named Wine Spectator Wine of the Year

wines.description: CVNE's prestige red from Rioja Alta fruit in top years. The 2004 was Wine Spectator Wine of the Year 2013, the first Spanish wine so honoured.

wines.name: Imperial Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 18 to 24 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: The Reserva tier of CVNE's prestige Imperial line, from Rioja Alta fruit: ripe, savoury and balanced with classic American-and-French-oak spice.

pairings.why: Vine-shoot-roasted suckling lamb is the canonical Rioja match; the wine's resolved tannins frame the sweet fat.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A thick aged-beef chop over embers meets the wine's savoury oak and grip.
Cured Iberian ham echoes the wine's dried-cherry and leather register.

history.summary: The Imperial Reserva is the younger-released sibling of the Gran Reserva, drawn from the same Rioja Alta sources.

milestones.event: Imperial line established

wines.description: The Reserva tier of CVNE's prestige Imperial line from Rioja Alta fruit. Ripe, savoury and balanced with classic oak spice.

wines.name: Cune Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 18 months in American and French oak

taste.summary: The house's widely available Reserva: classic, savoury and reliable, with American-oak spice over fresh Tempranillo fruit. A benchmark value Reserva.

pairings.why: Vine-shoot-roasted suckling lamb is the canonical Rioja match; the wine's resolved tannins frame the sweet fat.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A thick aged-beef chop over embers meets the wine's savoury oak and grip.
Cured Iberian ham echoes the wine's dried-cherry and leather register.

history.summary: Cune is the core brand of the house, named for the company initials, with the Reserva a consistent value classic.

milestones.event: Cune brand established with the founding of the company

wines.description: The house's widely available Reserva: classic, savoury and reliable, with American-oak spice over fresh Tempranillo. A benchmark value.

wines.name: Cune Crianza
wines.oak_regime: Around 12 months in American and French oak

taste.summary: The house's everyday Crianza: bright, juicy Tempranillo with gentle oak, a reliable weeknight Rioja and one of Spain's best-selling reds.

pairings.why: A juicy Crianza is the all-rounder for a varied pintxo bar.
Cider-braised chorizo and the wine's bright fruit are an easy match.
Simple grilled lamb meets the soft spice and supple tannin.

history.summary: The Cune Crianza is the gateway to the range, a high-volume everyday Rioja.

milestones.event: Cune brand established

wines.description: The house everyday Crianza: bright, juicy Tempranillo with gentle oak. A reliable weeknight Rioja and one of Spain's best-selling reds.

wines.name: Monopole Clasico
wines.oak_regime: Brief oak ageing with a small addition of manzanilla sherry in the classic style

taste.summary: A revival of the historic Monopole white blended with a touch of manzanilla sherry: saline, nutty and distinctive, a one-of-a-kind gastronomic white Rioja.

pairings.why: The salty anchovy-olive skewer is a perfect echo of the wine's manzanilla saline tang.
Marinated anchovy and the saline, nutty white are a Basque-coast classic.
Fried fresh anchovies meet the citrus and flor-like savour.

history.summary: Monopole is one of Spain's oldest white wine brands. The Clasico revives the historic recipe of blending in a little manzanilla sherry for a saline, savoury character.

milestones.event: Monopole established as a pioneering Spanish white brand
Monopole Clasico relaunched with the historic sherry-blend recipe

wines.description: A revival of the historic Monopole white blended with a touch of manzanilla sherry. Saline, nutty and a one-of-a-kind gastronomic white Rioja.

wines.name: Prado Enea Gran Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 12 months in vat then about 36 months in oak, with long bottle ageing, made only in the best years

taste.summary: Muga's traditional flagship, made only in great vintages with long oak ageing in the bodega's own cooperage; a deeply savoury, classical Gran Reserva of real longevity.

pairings.why: Vine-roast lamb is the textbook match for this grand traditional red.
Game and Prado Enea's truffle-and-tobacco depth align.
Aged-beef chop frames the fine, savoury tannin.
Hard sheep cheese sharpens the long savoury finish.

history.summary: Prado Enea is Muga's traditional prestige Gran Reserva, made only in the best vintages and aged for years in the bodega's own oak.

milestones.event: Prado Enea established as the house Gran Reserva

wines.description: Muga's traditional flagship, made only in great years with long oak ageing in the house cooperage. Deeply savoury and classical.

wines.name: Torre Muga
wines.oak_regime: Around 18 months in new French oak

taste.summary: Muga's modern, French-oak prestige red: dark, dense and polished, with mocha and cassis over firm tannins, a counterpoint to the traditional Prado Enea.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A char-grilled aged-beef chop matches Torre Muga's firm tannin and mocha oak.
Dark game meets the wine's cassis-and-espresso density.
Hard cheese balances the grippy structure.

history.summary: Torre Muga is the house's modern, internationally styled prestige red introduced in the early 1990s and aged in new French oak.

milestones.event: First Torre Muga vintage

wines.description: Muga's modern, French-oak prestige red: dark, dense and polished, with mocha and cassis over firm tannins.

wines.name: Aro
wines.oak_regime: Around 24 months in new French oak

taste.summary: Muga's icon wine, a tiny-production Tempranillo-Graciano from old vines aged in new French oak; concentrated, polished and ageworthy, the house's collector bottle.

pairings.why: Dark game meets Aro's concentrated cassis-and-graphite density.
A grand aged-beef chop for the house's icon wine.
Crystalline cheese sharpens the long mineral finish.

history.summary: Aro is Muga's icon wine, made in tiny quantities from selected old-vine parcels and aged in new French oak.

milestones.event: First Aro vintage as the house icon wine

wines.description: Muga's icon wine, a tiny-production Tempranillo-Graciano from old vines in new French oak. Concentrated, polished and ageworthy.

wines.name: Muga Seleccion Especial Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 26 months in French and American oak

taste.summary: A barrel selection above the standard Reserva, blending Muga's traditional and modern leanings into a ripe, polished but savoury Rioja.

pairings.why: Vine-shoot-roasted suckling lamb is the canonical Rioja match; the wine's resolved tannins frame the sweet fat.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A thick aged-beef chop over embers meets the wine's savoury oak and grip.
Cured Iberian ham echoes the wine's dried-cherry and leather register.

history.summary: The Seleccion Especial is a step up from the regular Muga Reserva, a barrel selection aged a little over two years in mixed oak.

milestones.event: Seleccion Especial introduced as a barrel-selection Reserva

wines.description: A barrel selection above the standard Reserva, blending Muga's traditional and modern leanings into a ripe, polished but savoury Rioja.

wines.name: Muga Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Around 24 months in oak, with racking and fining done in the traditional way

taste.summary: The house's calling-card Reserva, fermented in oak vats and aged in barrel; balanced, savoury and traditional, a benchmark mid-priced Rioja.

pairings.why: Vine-shoot-roasted suckling lamb is the canonical Rioja match; the wine's resolved tannins frame the sweet fat.

pairings.dish: Chuleton de buey a la brasa
pairings.why: A thick aged-beef chop over embers meets the wine's savoury oak and grip.
Cured Iberian ham echoes the wine's dried-cherry and leather register.

history.summary: The Muga Reserva is the house's core red and one of the most recognisable Rioja Reservas internationally, made in a traditional style with the bodega's own cooperage.

milestones.event: Bodegas Muga founded and the Reserva style established

wines.description: The house's calling-card Reserva, fermented in oak vats and aged in barrel. Balanced, savoury and traditional.
